title: iPhone Email Setup - Exchange Configuration
steps: Open Settings app on iPhone
Tap 'Mail' then 'Accounts'
Tap 'Add Account' then 'Microsoft Exchange'
Enter work email address
For server, use: outlook.office365.com
Enter work password
Tap 'Next' and wait for verification
Choose which items to sync (Mail, Contacts, Calendars)
Tap 'Save'
commonIssues: Server field shows wrong address - update to outlook.office365.com
Password incorrect - have customer verify with IT
Account already exists - delete old one first
scottsSOP: Always verify they're entering their WORK email, not personal. 90% of failures are wrong server address.
escalateIf: Customer doesn't know work email password

title: Identifying Phone Scams - Customer Education
steps: Reassure customer they did the right thing by calling
Explain: Apple never calls customers about viruses
Show them Settings > Privacy & Security > Safety Check
Review installed apps - look for anything unfamiliar
Check Safari for suspicious bookmarks or homepage changes
Enable automatic iOS updates in Settings > General > Software Update
redFlags: Phone call claiming to be from Apple Support
Request to install remote access apps
Demands for immediate payment or gift cards
scottsSOP: Never shame the customer. Praise them for being cautious. Most are embarrassed - make them feel smart for calling.
escalateIf: Customer already gave personal info to scammer
Suspicious apps installed that won't delete

title: Mr. Mac Internal Systems - HubSpot & Square
steps: Look up customer in HubSpot using phone number
Review previous issues and notes
Check Square Appointments for today's scheduled service
Update HubSpot with current issue and resolution
Set follow-up reminder if needed
Process payment in Square if repair completed
commonIssues: Customer not found in HubSpot - create new record
Previous notes unclear - ask Scott for clarification
Square appointment time doesn't match - verify with customer
scottsSOP: Always update HubSpot notes immediately after call. Future techs need to know what was tried.
escalateIf: HubSpot system is down or running slow
